Container.Overview: Ollama with Intel iGPU acceleration via IPEX-LLM (v1.1). Built from source using intel/oneapi-basekit — weekly auto-rebuilds pick up the latest ipex-llm and Ollama updates automatically. Runs LLM inference on Intel integrated graphics (Xe/Arc) using the SYCL backend. All model layers offloaded to GPU — no discrete GPU required. Supports Meteor Lake, Arrow Lake, and Intel Arc iGPUs. New: GPU device selection (ONEAPI_DEVICE_SELECTOR) and parallel request control (OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL).
Container.Changes:
[b]v1.1 (2026-04-16)[/b] - Rebuilt from source (intel/oneapi-basekit + pip install ipex-llm) - Weekly auto-rebuilds pick up latest ipex-llm and Ollama updates - Intel XPU PyTorch wheels (no NVIDIA CUDA bloat) - Added ONEAPI_DEVICE_SELECTOR for multi-GPU targeting - Added OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL for parallel request control - Ollama version printed on container startup
[b]v1.0 (2026-02-21)[/b] - Initial release with IPEX-LLM iGPU acceleration - SYCL backend for Intel Xe/Arc integrated graphics - Unraid template with br0 networking

Config.Name: Ollama Data
Config.Description: Ollama models, configuration, and SYCL shader cache

Config.Name: OLLAMA_NUM_GPU
Config.Description: Number of layers to offload to GPU (999 = all)

Config.Name: OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE
Config.Description: How long to keep models loaded after last request

Config.Name: ONEAPI_DEVICE_SELECTOR
Config.Description: Target specific GPU device (e.g., level_zero:0 for iGPU, level_zero:1 for dGPU). Leave empty to use all available GPUs.

Config.Name: OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL
Config.Description: Max parallel requests. Set to 1 for limited GPU memory. Leave empty for default.

Config.Name: SYCL_CACHE_PERSISTENT
Config.Description: Cache compiled SYCL shaders for faster subsequent loads

Config.Name: SYCL_PI_LEVEL_ZERO_USE_IMMEDIATE_COMMANDLISTS
Config.Description: Use immediate command lists for better performance
